title: Vice President Shri C.P Radhakrishnan Graces the 20th Convocation of Mizoram University
content:
538/2025-2026

Aizawl, the 7th March, 2026:Vice President of India Shri C.P. Radhakrishnan today graced the 20th Convocation of Mizoram University (MZU) as the Chief Guest. The ceremony was held at the Multipurpose Hall on the MZU campus in Tanhril, Aizawl. The event was also attended by the Governor of Mizoram and Chief Rector of Mizoram University, General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh (Retd), PVSM, AVSM, YSM, along with Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma, who served as Guests of Honour.

In his convocation address, Vice President Shri C.P. Radhakrishnan expressed his delight at being able to attend a convocation at Mizoram University, one of the premier institutions of higher education in Northeast India, and lauded the campus for its exceptional beauty, serene environment, and well-maintained infrastructure. He described the occasion as a cherished opportunity to interact with bright young minds who are destined to serve the nation, society, and humanity in the years ahead. He warmly congratulated all the graduating students and degree recipients, acknowledging the pivotal role played by their parents and teachers in their success. The Vice President emphasised that receiving a degree is not merely a certificate of completion but a certificate marking the beginning of a new and meaningful lifelong journey. He described the convocation not only as a joyous celebration but also as a solemn reminder of the fresh responsibilities that the graduates must now shoulder.

Highlighting the significance of peace, Shri C.P. Radhakrishnan pointed to the Mizoram Peace Accord of 1986 as one of India's most successful examples of conflict resolution through dialogue. He noted that "dialogue always triumphs over discord" and that education represents the most enduring outcome of peace, which is essential for sustainable development. He further spoke about the transformative impact of the Act East Policy on Northeast India, positioning the region as a dynamic gateway for India's engagement with Southeast Asia. Under the leadership of the Prime Minister, the Northeast has emerged as the heart of India's development agenda, he added. The Vice President described the inauguration of the Bairabi-Sairang railway line as a historic milestone, connecting Aizawl to the Indian railway network for the first time 78 years after Independence. Looking ahead to India's centenary of Independence in 2047, he affirmed the government's commitment to building a self-reliant and developed "Viksit Bharat." He stressed that this vision is not merely a government programme but a national mission in which the youth of the country, including today's graduates, will play a crucial role.

The Vice President urged the graduating students not to focus solely on seeking jobs but to aspire to become job creators and entrepreneurs. He encouraged them to shift from being opportunity seekers to opportunity creators. He also called on the youth of Northeast India to harness the region's vast untapped potential while confronting the menace of narcotic drug abuse with determination. He advised students to cultivate positive and optimistic thinking, master modern technologies, manage time effectively, and use social media responsibly. Praising Mizoram as India's most literate state and the one with the highest forest cover relative to its geographical area, Shri C.P. Radhakrishnan encouraged the youth to prioritize environmental sustainability alongside economic progress. He advocated for perseverance through self-motivation, avoiding shortcuts, dedicating time purposefully, and maintaining patience to achieve lasting success.

Governor General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh (Retd) described the convocation as a moment of culmination, inspiration, progress, and determination - a new beginning for continued advancement. He highlighted Mizoram University's remarkable growth since its inception, noting its strong academic profile, research contributions, and status today as a vibrant centre of higher learning in the Northeast, dedicated to scholarship, innovation, and community engagement. He commended the university for achieving an NAAC A+ grade and its contributions to both education and public service. In light of rapid technological changes, global interconnectedness, and complex societal challenges, he urged graduates to prioritize adaptability, innovation, and ethical clarity. The Governor stressed that a degree carries inherent responsibility and called on the students to use their knowledge and skills in service to others, embodying the spirit of selfless service - Tlawmngaihna.

Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma congratulated the successful graduates and extended gratitude to Vice President Shri C.P. Radhakrishnan for gracing the occasion. He outlined the state government's initiatives to advance education, including the upcoming launch of classes at Mizoram Engineering College and ongoing efforts to establish Mizoram State University. He highlighted the flagship "Handholding" scheme designed to empower youth with skills and self-reliance, urging graduates to make effective use of it. The Chief Minister also congratulated Mizoram University for securing the 82nd position among Indian universities in the NIRF 2025 rankings. He thanked all contributors to this achievement and extended best wishes for the University's future endeavours.

Earlier, MZU Registrar Prof. Lalnundanga, with permission of the Vice Chancellor, formally declared the convocation open. Mizoram University Vice-Chancellor Prof. Dibakar Chandra Deka delivered the welcome address. The ceremony included the conferment of degrees, gold medals, and book prizes. A total of 6,385 degrees were awarded under MZU. Of the 1,113 candidates who received their degrees in person, there were 147 Doctor of Philosophy recipients, 3 Master of Philosophy, 486 Master's Degree, 137 Master's Professional, 121 Bachelor's Degree, 174 Bachelor's Professional, 43 Gold Medalists, and 2 Book Prize winners.

Established in 2001, Mizoram University currently has 39 departments, 2 centres, and 10 Schools of Study offering UG, PG, and Ph.D. programmes. It also has 40 affiliated colleges and 1 constituent college.

Attachment

title: Vice President Pu C.P. Radhakrishnan in Mizoram University Convocation vawi 20-na hmanpui
content:
4729/2025-2026

Aizawl, the 7th March, 2026:Vice President Pu C.P. Radhakrishnan chuan vawiin khan Mizoram University (MZU) Convocation vawi 20-na, Multipurpose Hall, MZU Campus, Tanhrila neih chu Khuallian niin a hmanpui. He hunah hian Larsap General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh, PVSM, AVSM, YSM (Retd), Chief Rector of Mizoram University leh Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma te chu Khualzahawm an ni.

Vice President Pu C.P. Radhakrishnan chuan India hmarchhaka thiamna sang zawk zirna in tha leh campus nei tha berte zinga mi Mizoram University-a convocation a hmanpui thei chu lawmawm a tih thu sawiin, nakin lawka ram leh hnam bakah mahni mihring puite rawngbawl sak tur thalaite nena hun hman chu nuam a tih zawng a nih thu a sawi a. Hlawhtlinna chang zawng zawngte thinlung taka a lawmpui thu sawiin, an phena awm tlat nu leh pa leh zirtirtute chu fakawm a ti a. Degree chu zir zawh entirna certificate mai ni lo, hmathar lakna tur entirna certificate a nih thu a sawi a. Convocation chu hlimna hun leh kalzelna tura mawhphurhna thar lakna hun a ni, a ti bawk.

Vice President chuan India rama remna thuthlung ziah hlawhtling berte zinga mi Mizoram Peace Accord 1986 chuan inremlohna dawhkan hmanga chinfel theih a nihzia a lantir tih a sawi a. Zirna than zel nan muanna a pawimawh thu leh hmasawn nan ram ralmuang nih a tul thu a sawi a. Act East Policy-in hmarchhak state-ah danglamna nasa tak a thlen thu leh Asia chhim chhak ramte luhka pawimawh tak a nih tir thu a sawi a. Prime Minister kaihhruaina hnuaiah hmar chhak chu India rama hmasawnna bung pawimawh a nih thu sawiin, Bairabi-Sairang railway line hawn tak chuan ram zalen kan nih atanga kum 78 hnuah Indian railway map-ah Aizawl a chuantir a, mellung pawimawh tak a ni, a ti a. Zalenna hmuh atanga kum 100-na 2047 ah India chu ram intodelh leh hmasawn 'Viksit Bharat' a siam tumin sawrkarin theihtawp a chhuah tih sawiin, hei hi sawrkar programme mai a ni lova, kan zavaia tanrualna tur anih avangin thalaite'n mawhphurhna nasa tak an nei, a ti bawk.

Pu C.P. Radhakrishnan chuan thalaite chu hna zawngtu nih ngawt tum lova, a siam saktu nih tum tur leh, remchanna zawngtu aia siam saktu nih tum turin a fuih a. Hmarchhak state te'n theihna nasa tak an nei tih sawiin, thalaite hmelma lian ruihhlo erawh nasa zawka do tlan a tul tih a sawi a. Zirlaite chu a eng zawnga hmalam thlir turte, tunlai thiamna chhuak thar apiang thiam bel tum turte, hun vawng dik tur leh social media hman chin tawk hre turtein a chah a. India rama ziak leh chhiar thiam tamna ber leh a ram zau zawng atanga teha ramngaw nei tha a ni chu chhuanawm a tih thu sawiin, thalaite chu hmasawnna tlo leh ngelnghet, chhehvela thil awmte tibuai lo zawnga hma la turin a fuih bawk.

Larsap General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh (Retd) chuan convocation chu kum engemawchen inthununna, beih hram hramna, hmasawnna leh tumruhna ataka lan chhuahna hun mai bakah, hmasawn zel tura bultanna thar a ni, a ti a. MZU-in kawng hrang hranga hmasawnna a hmuh thu sawiin, tun dinhmunah chuan hmarchhak state-a zirna sang zawk zawm theihna ngaihhlut leh thang duang berte zinga mi a ni, a ti a. Mizoram University-in NAAC A+ grade a hmu chu lawmawm tiin, zirna bakah vantlang rawngbawlna kawnga an hmalaknate fakawm a ti a. Zirlaite chu khawvel hmasawn leh inthlak danglam zel mila insiamrem thiam turin a fuih a. Degree chuan mawhphurhna a kengtel tih sawiin, an thiamna leh finnate chu midang rawngbawl sak nana hmang tur leh Tlawmngaihna nunpui tlat thin turin a chah a ni.

Thusawitu dang Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chuan zirlai hlawhtling thar zawng zawngte a lawmpui thu sawiin, convocation hmanpui tura lokal Vice President Pu C.P. Radhakrishnan chungah lawmthu a sawi bawk a. Zirna tihhmasawn kawnga Mizoram sawrkar hmalaknate tarlangin, Mizoram Engineering College chu kumin atangin class tan tum a nih thu leh Mizoram State University kalpui tura hmalak zel a nih thu a sawi a. Mizoram sawrkar flagship scheme Handholding chu thalai, mahni kea ding tura theihna leh thiamna neite tana buatsaih a nih thu sawiin, scheme hmang tangkai turin thalaite a sawm a. University naupangte ni chunga Mizoram University-in NIFR 2025 ranking-ah India rama University tha 82-na a hauh pha chu chhuanawm tiin, hetiang dinhmun thleng thei tura thawktu zawng zawngte chungah lawmthu a sawi a, an kalzelna turah duhsakna a hlan a ni.

He hunah hian MZU Registrar Prof. Lalnundanga'n Vice Chancellor phalna laa convocation tan theih thu a puanfel hnuah Mizoram University Vice-Chancellor Prof. Dibakar Chandra Deka chuan kalkhawmte lawmna thu a sawi a. Degree, Gold Medal leh book prize winner lawmman hlanna hun hman a ni a. Registrar-in lawmthu sawiin Convocation a khar a ni.

MZU Convocation-ah hian MZU hnuaia zirlai 6385 te hnenah degree hlan a ni a. Vawiina inlan (candidates receiving degrees in person) 1113 zinga Doctor of Philosophy 147, Master of Philosophy 3, Master's Degree 486, Master's Professional 137, Bachelor's Degree 121, Bachelor's Professional 174, Gold Medalist 43 leh Book Prize dawngtu 2 te kutah degree leh lawmman hlan a ni.

Mizoram University hi kum 2001 khan din niin, tun dinhmunah Department 39 a awm a. Centre 2 te'n School of Study 10 ah UG, PG leh Ph.D programme an kalpui mek a. Affiliated College 40 leh Constituent College 1 a nei mek bawk.

Attachment

title: Mizoram State Commission for Women-Press Release On International Women’s Day, 2026 “Maha Jan Sunwai” Hmeichhiate harsatna tawk te tan FREE LEGAL PUBLIC HEARING NEIH TUR A NI
content:
4728/2025-2026

Dated Kolasib the 7th March 2026:International Women’s Day denchhen in National Commission for Women, New Delhi leh State Commission for Women chuan India Ram pumah “Maha Jan Sunwai” a buatsaih dawn a. International Women’s Day hi Kolasib District ah 10th March , 2026 ah hun hman tur a ni a. He hun hi Mizoram State Commission for Women mai bakah Kolasib Deputy Commissioner, Superintendent of Police, District Legal Services Authority leh Women and Child Development ten an buatsaihho dawn a ni.

He hun hi Prof. Rinpari Ralte, Member, Mizoram State Commission for Women in a hmanpui dawn a . Hmeichhia dikna chanvo rahbehna hrang hrang tuar te chuan he hunah hian complain a thehluh theih dawn a ni.

Inchhungkhura hleilenna, eng kawng pawha tawk te, nupa kar harsatna avanga hmeichhe chunga tawrhna thleng te, hnathawhna hmuna thawhpui mipate atanga harsatna tawk te, chhaih nawmnah tawkte leh eng kawng pawha hmeichhia nihna avanga hleilenna leh nek chepna tawk tan harsatna a thlen theih ang.

Free Legal Public hearing neih tur a nih angin Complain hi theh luh theih ani dawn a, thehlut duh te chuan District Programme Officer, WCD, Child Development Project Officer, WCD, Deputy Commissioner leh SP te hnen ah ziakin theh luh tur a ni. Complain theh lut te tan he mi ni hian a rang thei ang bera an harsatna te chinfel nghal tum a ni.

title: Serchhipah Maternal Death Review meeting neih a ni
content:
4727/2025-2026

Dated Serchhip, the 5th March, 2026:Nimin khan Serchhip DC Pu Paul L Khuma hoin DC Meeting Hall ah Health Department hotuten Maternal Death Review Meeting an nei.

Nu nau chunga thi Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR) hi India Sawrkar chuan tihniam tumin nasa takin tan a la a. State leh District tinah te hmalakna a kalpui mek a. Vanduaithlak takin Serchhip District chhung Khawbelah November 28, 2025 khan nu nau chunga thi pakhat a awm thu meeting ah hian ngaihthlak a ni a. He thil vanduaithlak tak hi engtin nge a lo thlen tih leh hetiang thil a thlen nawn loh nan engtin nge hmalak dan tur tih chungchang an sawiho a ni.

Meeting chuan mipuite hriselna enkawlna lama thawktu Health Care Providers te tana intuaitharna leh infuihtharna hun refresher training neih tir tha an ti a. Naupai laia thatlohna nei - high risk pregnancy nei mek te rang taka damdawi in an pan theih dan tur ngaihtuah chungchang te, hriselna ngai pawimawh kawngah mipuite hriatna tizau tura awareness campaign neih chungchang te an sawi ho a.

District chhung khaw thenkhat, damdawi in awm lohna hmun te leh damdawi in rang takq pan thei lo, harsatna neite chungchang pawh sawiho a ni a. DLAO kaltlangin hmanhmawh ngai bik emergency ah chuan Village Council te thawhpui dan ngaihtuah tha an ti a. Nu nau chunga thi an awm tawh loh nan, chak zawka hma lakna kalpui turin Sawrkar Department dangte nena thawhhona tha neih dan tur te an rel a ni.

He meeting ah hian Sr CMO Dr LP Malsawma bakah Health Department hotute, Serchhip DLAO leh Serchhip District thuneitute an tel a ni.

Attachment
